name: Qasit ibn Zuhayr
title: Taghlibi Brother
shortDescription: A sincere Shia and warrior of Kufa who escaped Ibn Ziyad's guards at night to join Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan.
fullStory:
Qasit ibn Zuhayr al-Taghlibi was one of the nobles, sincere Shias, and renowned warriors in Kufa. He was a companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and had fought alongside him in the battles of Jamal, Siffin, and Nahrawan.

When Imam Hussain (AS) reached Karbala, Qasit, along with his brave brothers Kurdus and Muqsit, decided to reach the Imam at any cost. Since Ibn Ziyad had blocked the roads, the three brothers left Kufa secretly at night and reached the Imam's tents before Ashura.

On the morning of Ashura, when the first collective attack of Umar Sa'd's army began, Qasit ibn Zuhayr and his brothers were at the front line of defense.

With unparalleled bravery and perfect coordination, they charged into the heart of the enemy army. After a fierce battle and killing a number of Kufans, they attained the great honor of martyrdom side by side in that very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى قاسِطٍ وَكُرْدُوسٍ ابْنَيْ زُهَيْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيَّيْنِ
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qasit and Kurdus, the sons of Zuhayr al-Taghlibi.
